Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1 - List Slack channels to identify the investigation channel and the engineering channel referenced in the prompt. Discovers C009 (#root-cause-aws-spike, topic: "Investigation: March 2026 AWS cost spike (~3x baseline)", created by moveops_samira_tariq, 5 members) and C003 (#engineering). Also discovers C005 (#finance) which becomes relevant when checking cross-functional follow-through. Tool: channels_list.
OE 2 - Retrieve message history of channel C009 (#root-cause-aws-spike) to trace the full March AWS cost investigation. Discovers the complete timeline: Samira opened the investigation (ts 1774993936, 2026-03-31) citing a March AWS bill of $42,870 versus a typical monthly baseline of approximately $14,300 (roughly 3x increase). Lena reported the billing breakdown (ts 1774998736): the autoscaling group moveops-api-prod scaled from 4 to 18 EC2 instances, the moveops-employee-docs S3 bucket saw approximately 47,000 document uploads in 7 days versus the usual 2,000 per month, and RDS database connections rose from an average of 50 to 340 with several slow queries in the relocation matching algorithm. Samira posted the RCA Summary of Resolution (ts 1775066236, 2026-04-01) listing items still in progress: AWS cost alert thresholds (ETA Wednesday), cost tagging per customer (ETA Wednesday), pricing model review meeting scheduled for next week, and infrastructure cost estimation tool at P1 priority. Marcus replied (ts 1775066536) acknowledging the summary and emphasizing the pricing review meeting must produce clear guidelines. Tool: conversations_history with channel_id C009 for channel-level messages; conversations_replies with channel_id C009 and thread_ts 1775066236.000000 for Marcus's threaded reply.
OE 3 - Retrieve thread replies on Samira's recap message (thread_ts 1775000836.000000) in C009 to discover the root cause identification chain. Finds Marcus asking Julian about major enterprise onboarding around 3/21 (ts 1775001556), Julian identifying Vantage Distributed as the cause -- approximately 500 employees starting Monday March 21 with the remaining roughly 700 including contractors by March 23, over 1,200 users total accessing the portal, part of a $1,850,000 per year agreement (ts 1775002036). Also finds Marcus demanding infrastructure cost estimates before pricing enterprise deals and requesting a meeting with Elena (ts 1775004136). Tool: conversations_replies with channel_id C009 and thread_ts 1775000836.000000.
OE 4 - Retrieve message history of channel C003 (#engineering) to find Samira's post-RCA engineering action plan. Discovers Samira's message (ts 1775059936, 2026-04-01) breaking work into three priority tiers: P0 This Week -- (1) AWS cost alert thresholds at 50%, 100%, and 200% of monthly baseline via CloudWatch plus Slack integration to #finance, (2) budget tags for all key AWS resources for cost allocation by customer. P1 Next 2 Weeks -- (3) infrastructure cost estimator tool for sales to estimate AWS costs per employee, (4) relocation matching algorithm slow query optimization (Lena discovered these during investigation). P2 This Quarter -- (5) autoscaling policy review for spot instances on burst traffic, (6) S3 lifecycle policies for onboarding documents. Samira directly asks Lena to take items 1-2 this week. Tool: conversations_history with channel_id C003.
OE 5 - Retrieve thread replies on Samira's engineering action plan (thread_ts 1775059936.000000) in C003 to find Lena's commitment. Discovers Lena's reply (ts 1775062636, 2026-04-01) confirming she has begun setting up CloudWatch alarms and expects items 1 and 2 ready by Wednesday. Specifically: a Lambda function checking Cost Explorer daily to notify #finance if thresholds are surpassed, and budget tags added via a Terraform update tagged by customer ID. Lena also states she found three slow queries in the relocation matching algorithm that are not utilizing indexes correctly and describes this as a quick fix she can address concurrently. Tool: conversations_replies with channel_id C003 and thread_ts 1775059936.000000.
OE 6 - Search emails for Elena's post-RCA directives. Discovers email-rootcause-002 from elena.rostova@moveops.com to samira.tariq@moveops.com and marcus.thorne@moveops.com (CC: julian.brooks@moveops.com, catalina.dubois@moveops.com, lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com, alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com), subject "Re: Root Cause Analysis: March AWS Cost Spike - Vantage Distributed Onboarding." Elena's directives cover more than just engineering: (1) Samira must implement AWS cost alert thresholds this week (non-negotiable) and prioritize the infrastructure cost estimator tool before the next enterprise pitch, (2) Marcus must arrange a pricing model review meeting for next week with Samira, Julian, and Catalina to build a cost model per employee, (3) Julian must ensure any deal exceeding 500 employees requires engineering cost approval before pricing is finalized, (4) leadership meeting on Monday to discuss. Also discovers Samira's formal RCA email (email-rootcause-001) and Marcus's original escalation email in the same thread. Tool: search_emails with query related to "root cause" or "AWS" or "cost spike" (or similar).
OE 7 - Search Linear for any existing tickets related to AWS cost controls, cost alerts, budget tags, slow query optimization, or infrastructure cost estimator. Discovers that zero tickets exist for any of these items -- all six engineering action items from Samira's P0/P1/P2 breakdown were never tracked in Linear and fell off entirely. Also discovers ENG-187 (id linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2, number 187, title "Invoice email template safety checks - prevent attachment mismatches," assigned to moveops_lena_bjorkstrom, state_backlog, priority 2) which references "Terraform pricing leak incident" in its description and could initially appear related. Tool: linear_list_issues with team team_engineering and/or query related to "cost" or "AWS" (or similar).
OE 8 - Retrieve full details of ENG-187 (linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2) to determine whether it is related to the AWS cost controls work. Reads the complete description and confirms ENG-187 is about validating that invoice email PDF attachments match the selected QuickBooks invoice, customer, and recipient account -- a safeguard following a pricing leak incident where wrong PDFs were sent. It is entirely unrelated to AWS cost alerts, budget tags, slow queries, or infrastructure cost estimation. No existing tickets require updating for the AWS cost control work. Tool: linear_get_issue with id linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2.
OE 9 - Search CRM for the client referenced in the prompt as starting their first relocation batch. The agent already knows from the Slack investigation (OE 2-3) that the company causing the March spike was "Vantage Distributed," and the prompt refers to "Terraform." Searching crm_search_companies with name "Vantage Distributed" or "Vantage" (or similar) returns the company record with id company-terraform-digital, name "Vantage Distributed", type CUSTOMER, 1,200 employees, domain vantagedistributed.com, industry Technology, created 2026-03-18, primary contact contact-terraform-primary. The critical discovery is that the CRM id contains "terraform-digital" while the company name is "Vantage Distributed" -- confirming that "Terraform" in the prompt and "Vantage Distributed" from the investigation are the same entity. The upcoming relocation batch will generate additional load from the same client that already triggered the 3x cost surge, making the missing cost controls an immediate risk. Tool: crm_search_companies with name "Vantage Distributed" or "Vantage" or "Terraform" (or similar).
OE 10 - Search calendar for the pricing model review meeting that Elena directed Marcus to arrange for next week. Discovers that no such meeting exists -- it was never scheduled despite Elena's directive in email-rootcause-002, Samira's RCA Summary claiming "Meeting scheduled for next week to review the pricing model" in C009, and Marcus's post in #finance (C005) stating "I will send out calendar invites for the review meeting." This is a gap in the cross-functional follow-through. Tool: calendar_search_events with query "pricing review" or "pricing model" (or similar).
OE 11 - Search Slack channel C005 (#finance) for Marcus's follow-up on the pricing review. Discovers Marcus's post (ts 1775068036, 2026-04-01) outlining a four-item agenda: (1) cost model per employee for infrastructure, (2) 500-employee threshold for engineering cost approval per Elena's request, (3) margin evaluation on Vantage Distributed with Alejandro, (4) continuous monthly cost tracking. Marcus states "I will send out calendar invites for the review meeting." Alejandro replies (ts 1775068516) with 6-month AWS cost comparison: October through February steady at approximately $14,300 per month (with December slightly higher at $15,200) and March at $42,870. Despite planning, the meeting was never calendared. Tool: conversations_search_messages with filter_in_channel C005 and search_query related to "pricing review" or "pricing model" (or similar), or conversations_history with channel_id C005.
OE 12 - Look up Marcus Thorne's contact details to obtain his email address before sending the status email. Discovers contact_id moveops_marcus_thorne, email marcus.thorne@moveops.com, job Head of Finance, phone (415) 959-8010. Tool: contacts_search_contacts with query "Marcus" or "Marcus Thorne" (or similar).
OE 13 - Create a Linear ticket for AWS cost alert thresholds (P0 item 1 from Samira's action plan). Lena committed to this but it was never tracked. Title related to "AWS cost alert thresholds" or "CloudWatch cost monitoring alerts" (or similar), team team_engineering, assignee moveops_lena_bjorkstrom, priority 1 (urgent). Description should reference CloudWatch alarms at 50%, 100%, and 200% of monthly baseline with Slack integration to #finance via Lambda checking Cost Explorer daily. Tool: linear_create_issue with team team_engineering, title (or similar), assignee moveops_lena_bjorkstrom, priority 1.
OE 14 - Create a Linear ticket for budget tags per customer (P0 item 2 from Samira's action plan). Lena committed to this but it was never tracked. Title related to "AWS budget tags" or "cost allocation tags by customer" (or similar), team team_engineering, assignee moveops_lena_bjorkstrom, priority 1 (urgent). Description should reference Terraform-based tagging by customer ID so finance can track costs per customer. Tool: linear_create_issue with team team_engineering, title (or similar), assignee moveops_lena_bjorkstrom, priority 1.
OE 15 - Create a Linear ticket for relocation matching algorithm slow query optimization (P1 item 4 from Samira's action plan). Lena identified three slow queries during the investigation and committed to fixing them concurrently. Title related to "Relocation matching slow query optimization" or "RDS slow query fix" (or similar), team team_engineering, assignee moveops_lena_bjorkstrom. Description should reference three queries not utilizing indexes correctly in the relocation matching algorithm, contributing to the RDS connection spike during Vantage Distributed onboarding. Tool: linear_create_issue with team team_engineering, title (or similar), assignee moveops_lena_bjorkstrom.
OE 16 - Create a Linear ticket for the infrastructure cost estimator tool (P1 item 3 from Samira's action plan, also directly mandated by Elena). Title related to "Infrastructure cost estimator tool" or "AWS cost per employee calculator for sales" (or similar), team team_engineering. Elena directed Samira to prioritize this so Julian and the sales team can use it before the next enterprise pitch. Tool: linear_create_issue with team team_engineering, title (or similar).
OE 17 - Create a Linear ticket for the autoscaling policy review (P2 item 5 from Samira's action plan). This item was never tracked in Linear. Title related to "Autoscaling policy review - spot instances for burst traffic" (or similar), team team_engineering. Description should reference reviewing the autoscaling configuration that scaled moveops-api-prod from 4 to 18 instances during the Vantage Distributed onboarding, and assessing spot instance usage for handling burst traffic from large enterprise onboardings. Tool: linear_create_issue with team team_engineering, title (or similar).
OE 18 - Create a Linear ticket for S3 lifecycle policies for onboarding documents (P2 item 6 from Samira's action plan). This item was never tracked in Linear. Title related to "S3 lifecycle policies for onboarding documents" (or similar), team team_engineering. Description should reference establishing lifecycle policies for the moveops-employee-docs S3 bucket to manage the volume of document uploads during enterprise onboardings (47,000 uploads in 7 days during Vantage Distributed onboarding versus the usual 2,000 per month). Tool: linear_create_issue with team team_engineering, title (or similar).
OE 19 - Post the full status picture to channel C003 (#engineering) as Lena. The message should cover: summary of investigation findings (Vantage Distributed onboarding caused 3x AWS spike, $42,870 vs $14,300 baseline), the CRM discovery that Terraform in CRM is actually Vantage Distributed (same client starting a new relocation batch), confirmation that none of the six committed engineering items were ever tracked in Linear, the status of Elena's cross-functional directives (pricing review meeting never scheduled on calendar, 500-employee engineering cost approval threshold status unclear), the new tickets created for all six engineering items (P0 through P2), and the urgency given Terraform/Vantage Distributed is about to start their first relocation batch without cost controls in place. Tool: conversations_add_message with channel_id C003, user_id moveops_lena_bjorkstrom.
OE 20 - Send email to Marcus Thorne since he escalated the original AWS cost issue. Sender lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com, recipient marcus.thorne@moveops.com. The email should cover: the full status of engineering follow-through on the March AWS cost spike (all committed work on cost alerts, budget tags, and slow queries fell off and was never tracked in Linear), the entity connection between Terraform in CRM and Vantage Distributed (same client that caused the spike), the risk that Terraform/Vantage Distributed is starting their first relocation batch without cost controls, the pricing review meeting Elena directed Marcus to arrange was never put on the calendar, the new Linear tickets created to close all six engineering gaps (P0 through P2), and the current status of Elena's other directives. Tool: send_email with sender lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com, recipients marcus.thorne@moveops.com.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

OE 16 - Create a Linear ticket for the infrastructure cost estimator tool (P1 item 3 from Samira's action plan, also directly mandated by Elena). OE 17 - Create a Linear ticket for the autoscaling policy review (P2 item 5 from Samira's action plan). This item was never tracked in Linear. Title related to "Autoscaling policy review - spot instances for burst traffic" (or similar), team team_engineering. OE 18 - Create a Linear ticket for S3 lifecycle policies for onboarding documents (P2 item 6 from Samira's action plan). This item was never tracked in Linear. Title related to "S3 lifecycle policies for onboarding documents" (or similar), team team_engineering. Description should reference establishing lifecycle policies for the moveops-employee-docs S3 bucket to manage the volume of document uploads during enterprise onboardings (47,000 uploads in 7 days during Vantage Distributed onboarding versus the usual 2,000 per month). Tool: linear_create_issue with team team_engineering, title (or similar).
OE 19 - Post the full status picture to channel C003 (#engineering) as Lena. The message should cover: summary of investigation findings (Vantage Distributed onboarding caused 3x AWS spike, $42,870 vs $14,300 baseline), the CRM discovery that Terraform in CRM is actually Vantage Distributed (same client starting a new relocation batch), confirmation that none of the six committed engineering items were ever tracked in Linear, the status of Elena's cross-functional directives (pricing review meeting never scheduled on calendar, 500-employee engineering cost approval threshold status unclear), the new tickets created for all six engineering items (P0 through P2), and the urgency given Terraform/Vantage Distributed is about to start their first relocation batch without cost controls in place. Tool: conversations_add_message with channel_id C003, user_id moveops_lena_bjorkstrom.
OE 20 - Send email to Marcus Thorne since he escalated the original AWS cost issue. Sender lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com, recipient marcus.thorne@moveops.com. The email should cover: the full status of engineering follow-through on the March AWS cost spike (all committed work on cost alerts, budget tags, and slow queries fell off and was never tracked in Linear), the entity connection between Terraform in CRM and Vantage Distributed (same client that caused the spike), the risk that Terraform/Vantage Distributed is starting their first relocation batch without cost controls, the pricing review meeting Elena directed Marcus to arrange was never put on the calendar, the new Linear tickets created to close all six engineering gaps (P0 through P2), and the current status of Elena's other directives. Tool: send_email with sender lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com, recipients marcus.thorne@moveops.com.
